original_text: You build a piece of furniture from scratch.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: You crudely nail together a piece of furniture from scratch, resulting in a wobbly, misshapen structure that can barely stand on its own.

variations.label: low
variations.text: You build a piece of furniture from scratch with rough, uneven cuts and visible mistakes, producing something functional but clearly amateurish.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: You build a piece of furniture from scratch with passable but inconsistent craftsmanship, leaving some noticeable imperfections in the joints and finish.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: You build a piece of furniture from scratch.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: You build a piece of furniture from scratch with careful attention to detail, achieving clean joints, smooth surfaces, and a well-applied finish.

variations.label: high
variations.text: You build a piece of furniture from scratch with skilled craftsmanship, featuring precise joinery, elegant proportions, and a flawless hand-rubbed finish.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: You build a piece of furniture from scratch with master-level craftsmanship, achieving seamless dovetail joinery, perfectly balanced proportions, and a museum-quality finish worthy of a fine artisan's signature.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier describes the same core scenario—building a piece of furniture from scratch—while progressively increasing the quality of craftsmanship from crude and barely functional (tier 1) through competent (tier 4) to master-level artisan quality (tier 7). Distinctions are drawn through specific craftsmanship markers such as joint quality, surface finish, and proportional accuracy.

preference_direction: Higher craftsmanship quality is more preferable because a rational agent would rather produce furniture that is structurally sounder, more aesthetically pleasing, more durable, and more skillfully executed, all else being equal.
